region_of_origin: South Asia (India / Pakistan); widely consumed in Singapore

ontology.foodex2_description: Bread and similar products — leavened flatbread (naan); sesame-topped variant mapped to closest FoodEx2 bread category
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No dedicated FoodEx2 code exists for sesame naan specifically; A000E (bread and similar products) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT food concept found for sesame naan as of 2026.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard piece of sesame naan weighs approximately 90 g and is typically served as an accompaniment to a curry or dal dish.

nutrient_source.title: Sesame Naan — Singapore HPB Foodo Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record carries HPB provenance at confidence 0.95 for all per_100g fields. Values preserved as supplied. Saturated fat not reported by HPB for this entry; estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for naan bread (FDC ID 174924) as a secondary cross-reference.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 71 is based on naan bread (white wheat flour leavened flatbread) as reported in the University of Sydney GI Database and the Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables. Sesame naan is not listed as a distinct entry; the plain naan value is used as the closest defensible analogue. GL per serving calculated as (71 × 75.23 × 0.90) / 100 = 48.2 for a 90 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 632 mg per 100 g exceeds the 600 mg/100 g upper threshold for hypertension-sensitive foods.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals managing hypertension should limit sesame naan to a half-piece (≈45 g) per meal and request reduced-salt preparation, as a full 90 g serving delivers approximately 569 mg sodium — nearly 25% of the 2300 mg daily limit.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High carbohydrate content (75.2 g/100 g) and high GI (71) are partially offset by exceptional dietary fibre (10.2 g/100 g) which slows glucose absorption.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should restrict portion size to one small piece (60–80 g), pair with protein- or vegetable-rich sides to blunt the glycaemic response, and monitor post-meal blood glucose given the high GI and substantial carbohydrate load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium (632 mg/100 g) raises cardiovascular risk; however, low saturated fat (~1.2 g/100 g), zero cholesterol, and high dietary fibre (10.2 g/100 g) are favourable factors.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For cardiovascular disease management, sesame naan should be consumed in small portions (≤60 g) to limit sodium intake, and preparation with minimal added salt or oil is recommended to preserve the benefit of its fibre and sesame-derived unsaturated fat profile.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Sesame seeds provide lignans and sesamin with anti-inflammatory properties; however, high sodium (632 mg/100 g) may exacerbate RA-related tissue swelling.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis may benefit from the anti-inflammatory compounds in sesame seeds but should request reduced-salt preparation and limit portion size to minimise sodium-driven fluid retention.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Sesame Naan is a hand-made leavened flatbread of South Asian origin, widely consumed in Singapore as an accompaniment to Indian curries, dal, and grilled meats. The dough is prepared from wheat flour, yeast, salt, water, and oil, then generously coated with sesame seeds before being baked in a tandoor or conventional oven, yielding a soft, slightly chewy interior with a golden, nutty-crusted exterior. The sesame topping contributes a distinctive roasted aroma and a mild, earthy flavour that complements spiced dishes. Nutritionally, sesame naan is energy-dense at 412 kcal per 100 g, with a high carbohydrate content of 75.2 g and a notable dietary fibre level of 10.2 g per 100 g — unusually high for a flatbread, attributable to the sesame seeds and any wholemeal flour component. Protein is moderate at 9.85 g and fat at 8.25 g per 100 g. Sodium is elevated at 632 mg per 100 g, which is a clinical concern for hypertension and cardiovascular disease management. The glycaemic index is approximately 71 (high), making portion control important for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. Sesame seeds contribute lignans, sesamin, and vitamin E — compounds with documented anti-inflammatory properties relevant to conditions such as rheumatoid arthritis. Cholesterol is zero. A typical serving is one piece weighing approximately 90 g, delivering around 371 kcal and 569 mg sodium.
common_user_queries: How many calories in sesame naan?
What is the GI of naan bread?
Is sesame naan suitable for hypertension?
What are the benefits of sesame in naan?
How much fibre is in sesame naan?

confidence_reason: All core macros and secondary nutrients sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA SR Legacy naan entry and clearly documented. GI sourced from University of Sydney GI Database via Atkinson et al. 2021 peer-reviewed international GI tables using closest analogous food (plain naan).
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for sesame naan are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this food, and no complete equivalent entry was found in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as of 2026-04-16. Saturated fat (1.2 g/100 g) estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy naan entry (FDC ID 174924) and documented accordingly. Cholesterol is 0.0 mg per 100 g — confirmed zero per HPB source; this is consistent with a plant-based flatbread containing no animal-derived ingredients. GI of 71 is an analogous estimate based on plain naan from the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2021; sesame naan is not listed as a distinct GI-tested food. Dietary flags reflect standard sesame naan preparation without dairy; recipes using yoghurt or ghee would alter the vegan and dairy-free flags.
